enrichment.summary: Kyle vlogs from Boulder on December 9, 2016, describing his late-night work schedule and excitement over discovering the Naumachia of Augustus on a historical map of Rome he is using as reference. He is building a 3D recreation of ancient Rome and recalibrates his timeline, now planning to work on the project until early February after realizing it is far larger than expected.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Vastly underestimated how long the Rome recreation will take; now committing ~10 weeks through early February
Finds it fascinating and is excited to build it; loves that Boulder has a lake of the same size
Wants the entire area within the Aurelian Wall to look like one cohesive 3D city, even buildings not on the source map
Usually limits himself to 2-3 weeks on a project, but will extend to months if it is mind-blowingly interesting

transcript.text: Hello, living in Boulder Vlog. Right when I hit this video, this button, my washing machine started going.
So if I had known, I wouldn't have done it. This is the one for Friday, December 9th.
Yeah, so yesterday, I was working until 6 in the morning.
I went to bed right as it was getting light. I like to go to sleep before it gets light, so I can feel like I'm sleeping at night.
But I got up at noon or something, noon one, so I was 67 hours of sleep.
And so yesterday, I discovered the Namatia. I didn't even know the Namatia was there, because I hadn't looked at it.
It takes a long time to notice buildings when there's a lot of them in the city, but I'm going to drag this to the side so I don't distract myself.
So, the Namatia of Augustus, the Namatia, the water battles, Augustus made a 500 meter diameter lake that he had 30 boats go on with 3,000 prisoners of war, all fight to the death.
So, it's where the saying, for those about to die, we salute you, comes from, originally, I think.
Yeah, so that's pretty trippy.
And it's the same exact size as this lake up here, so Boulder has our twin lake to the Namatia.
And also, the other lake inside of, there's another, like, man-made palace lake in Rome.
It's the same size as this other lake that used to live here, so that's cool.
But, yeah, so the, I was saying, and also yesterday, I started working on Serbian Wall.
I deleted my Serbian Wall and started working on the Serbian Wall from this new map, because it's better.
So, yeah, I mean, it would take at least a whole day just to do the Serbian Wall and at least a whole day just to do the Namatia.
I mean, maybe if I can finish the Namatia today, that'd be cool.
So, yeah, to be done by Sunday is ridiculous.
I don't know, I mean, Monday, I don't know what the hell I was thinking.
It was because I didn't, I just didn't notice how big it was.
And also, I also was looking at the map yesterday, and I noticed that once I'm done with all these buildings that I'm working on,
That they're on the map in the area, and then I build them, that's only half of the territory of the Aurelian Wall.
Like, I'm going to have to fill another half of it up with my own buildings.
And if I want to make it all look 3D, which I do, I really do want to make it all look one city within the Aurelian Wall.
If I'm going to do that and make all the other new buildings look like the old buildings, that's going to take a fucking long-ass time.
So, I think I'll just hammer until the beginning of February.
I mean, I like to make a rule, I only work on something for 2 or 3 weeks,
But if it's something that's, like, mind-blowingly interesting, then I could put more, I could put months into it.
So, I'll go 10 weeks from this, 2 1⁄2 months, because that's how I want to take those other guys.
And so, I'll see what I can do, and then I'll probably bail and work on something else.
But, the Nomatia only existed for a little bit in the time of Augustus, like, you know, like 15 AD or whatever, on the time of Jesus.
So, this guy who put this map together in 1840, they kind of put all of the greatest hits,
But not exactly how it looked in one particular year.
So, I want to do my request video about Courage, whoo, later on today, but I got to, I'm still running ahead on going on this Nomatia thing.
I'm really excited, because I wanted to know where it was, I knew about it, and I didn't know that it was on this map, so it's awesome.
And it's all within the, we're all in the wall, too.
So, anyways, I think that's enough talking for now, I'm going to get back to the project.
Ciao.
